avg.description: Calculates the average number of characters (graphemes) and bytes per non-empty line in a file.

bg.description: Generates a structured JSONL request batch from a source text file for translation tasks.

flags.context: Additional context or a file path containing context to include in the prompt for each chunk.
flags.model: Overrides the model specified in the parameter set.
flags.params: Parameter set to use for prompt and model configuration. (default: {{ .DefaultModel }})
flags.reason: Toggle reasoning mode for presets that support it (inverts the default setting).
flags.regex: Regular expression pattern used to segment the source file into chunks, instead of line-based chunk size.

bg2.description: Generates a structured JSONL request batch for general text transformations. If the source file is omitted or set to '-', standard input (stdin) is processed.

flags.model: Overrides the model specified in the parameter set.
flags.params: Parameter set to use for prompt and model configuration. (default: {{ .DefaultModel }})
flags.prompt: The prompt (or file path containing the prompt) to prepend to each chunk. Overrides the preset's prepend prompt.
flags.pselector: Lookup and use a prompt preset from the configuration templates as the instruction.
flags.reason: Toggle reasoning mode for presets that support it (inverts the default setting).
flags.sysprompt: System prompt or a file path containing the system prompt to use. Overrides the preset's system prompt.

br.description: Executes a JSONL batch job natively, managing retries and structured outputs, or exports a completed JSONL to plain text. If the target path is omitted, it is automatically derived from the source path.

flags.export: Export an already completed output JSONL file to a standard text file.
flags.params: Model preset to pull settings from (default: {{ .DefaultModel }}).
flags.reason: Toggle reasoning mode for presets that support it (inverts the default setting).
flags.retry: Retry only the failed requests from a previous run. Takes the target output file of the retry run as an argument.
flags.stream: Enables real-time streaming to the terminal if batch size is 1. (Note: Streaming bypasses backend metadata preservation).
flags.url: Base API URL (appends JSONL endpoint automatically).

cfg.description: Manages the application configuration and active locale.

flags.edit: Open the user configuration file in the default editor.
flags.remove: Delete the user configuration files (prompts for confirmation).

cm.description: Compares and merges two text files side-by-side into a single output file.

flags.force: Force the merge even if the two input files have a different number of lines.
flags.width: Set the width (in columns) for each half of the output. (default: {{ .Width }})

jd.description: Compares structured JSON translation keys, merges new translations, or safely splits files while preserving hierarchical paths.

flags.merge: Updates the target JSON in-place with localized values from a secondary translation map.
flags.size: Maximum size in KB of each split file (defaults to 20).
flags.split: Safely splits a hierarchical JSON file into multiple parts while preserving object path structures.

mg.description: Recursively finds and merges text files from a source directory. Supports glob filtering via flags or .mginclude/.mgignore files.

flags.exclude: Glob patterns to exclude (colon-separated, e.g., "tests/**:node_modules/**") or a path to a file containing patterns.
flags.extension: The file extension to look for (required).
flags.failed: Merges successful retries back into the original output file. Requires <original_jsonl> <retry_jsonl> [output_file].
flags.include: Glob patterns to include (colon-separated, e.g., "src/**:libs/**") or a path to a file containing patterns.
flags.noignore: Do not read or apply rules from local .mgignore files.
flags.params: The parameter config preset to use for resolving target strategy and JSONL formats.

or.description: Fetches and displays OpenRouter API key limits, daily usage, and remaining credits.

flags.apikey: The OpenRouter API key to query. Overrides all other key configurations.
flags.help: Display help information for this command.
flags.params: Preset configuration key used to look up the API key fallback.

os.description: Executes a single, one-shot prompt against an LLM. The prompt can be literal text or a file path. Supports context from standard input (stdin), text files, images, and audio.

flags.apikey: Provide an API key for the request.
flags.audio: Path to an audio file for audio context. Supported formats: mp3, mp4, mpeg, mpga, m4a, wav, webm.
flags.chunksize: The chunk size used to truncate the input text.
flags.file: Path to a text file to be appended to the prompt as context.
flags.image: Path to image(s) for visual context. Supports glob patterns and colon-separated lists (e.g., "img1.jpg:*.png"). Supported formats: png, jpg/jpeg, gif, webp.
flags.model: Override the model name specified in the preset.
flags.outfile: Path to save the LLM's output, offering an alternative to the positional [output_file] argument.
flags.partial: Lookup a prompt preset and truncate the input text to the first chunk of lines.
flags.pselector: Lookup and use a prompt preset from the configuration templates as the instruction.
flags.reason: Toggle reasoning mode for presets that support it (inverts the default setting).

rd.description: Launches a local web server to display a text file in a paginated, distraction-free reader.

rm.description: Manages and removes downloaded model tokenizers and configuration files.

flags.all: Remove all targeted models or binaries.

sp.description: Splits a large text file into smaller parts without breaking lines. If the target directory is omitted, a subfolder is automatically created.

flags.size: The maximum size of each part in megabytes (default: {{ .Size }}).

st.description: Strips or extracts content between specified delimiters. Reads from standard input (stdin) if '-' is used, and outputs to stdout if the target path is omitted.

flags.brainrot: Normalizes paragraph spacing to double newlines.
flags.compress: Compresses and removes consecutive empty lines in the output.
flags.extracttag: Extract content between delimiters instead of removing it.
flags.params: Use start/end delimiters from a preset.

tc.description: Counts the exact number of tokens in a source JSONL file using a model-specific downloaded tokenizer.

flags.params: The model preset whose tokenizer to use (default: {{ .DefaultModel }}).

tc2.description: Calculate token consumption statistics for a plaintext file split into consecutive line-bounded chunks.

flags.chunksize: Manual line override limit for each processing chunk segment.
flags.params: The target tokenizer configuration profile to load and execute calculations with.

commandDescriptions.avg: Calculates average line length of a file.
commandDescriptions.bg: Generates a JSONL batch file for translation prompts.
commandDescriptions.bg2: Generates a JSONL batch file for generic transformation prompts.
commandDescriptions.br: Native JSONL batch runner and exporter.
commandDescriptions.cm: Compares and merges two files side-by-side.
commandDescriptions.jd: Analyzes, merges, or splits structured JSON files.
commandDescriptions.mg: Recursively merges text files from a directory.
commandDescriptions.or: Displays OpenRouter API key limits and statistics.
commandDescriptions.os: Executes a single prompt with optional file/media context.
commandDescriptions.rd: Launches a local web server for paginated reading.
commandDescriptions.sp: Splits a large file into smaller parts by size.
commandDescriptions.st: Strips or extracts text between delimiters.
commandDescriptions.tc: Counts tokens in a JSONL batch request file.
commandDescriptions.tc2: Counts tokens in a raw plaintext file by segment chunks.

generic.footer: For more information on any command, use `telocity <command> --help`.
generic.header: telocity: A tool for batch processing text with LLMs.

languages._LLM_INSTRUCTIONS_ALIASES:
RULES FOR GENERATING ALIASES:
For each language entry, expand the 'aliases' array with a comprehensive list of lowercase search terms a user speaking the target language might input. Include:
1. CODES: Standard 2-letter and 3-letter ISO codes (e.g., 'ja', 'jpn', 'fr', 'fra').
2. NAMES: The language name in English, in its native script (e.g., '日本語', 'Español'), and in the target translation language (e.g., 'フランス語').
3. REGIONS: The base country or region name in the target translation language (e.g., 'フランス', '西班牙', 'spain').
4. TYPING & SCRIPTS: For non-Latin scripts, include standard romanizations and keyboard IME input strings (e.g., pinyin 'xibanyayu', 'zhongwen'; hiragana/romaji 'にほんご', 'nihongo'). For Latin scripts, include accent-stripped variants (e.g., 'espanol', 'francais').
5. ALTERNATES: Common alternate naming conventions or regional dialects (e.g., 'mandarin', 'castellano').
Ensure all values are lowercase, unique, strictly relevant, and linguistically authentic to the target language context. Avoid cross-language contamination and phonetic translation errors (e.g., do not translate Tagalog as '他ガログ').

cm.success: Successfully generated side-by-side text out of {{ .Lines }} line segments into -> {{ .Target }}

jd.fullyAligned: Both JSON structures are fully aligned. No missing keys found.
jd.mergedSuccess: Successfully merged translation keys from '{{ .TranslationFile }}' into '{{ .TargetFile }}'.
jd.savedSuccess: Successfully saved missing keys output to '{{ .OutputPath }}'.
jd.splitSuccess: Successfully split JSON file into {{ .Count }} parts.

models.completion: Legacy v1/completions preset. Edit the configuration file to customize.
models.effort: Reasoning preset with effort parameter support.
models.fallback: A generic preset for any OpenAI-compatible API.
models.genericprep: A generic preset that prepends the prompt to the user's text.
models.genericsys: A generic preset that uses a system prompt.
models.noHelp: No help text available for this preset.

lllm.processingComplete: Processing for this file is already complete.
lllm.quittingWithoutSaving: Force quitting. Saving partial progress and marking remaining requests as failed.
